steps.voice: A short tap anywhere on the dashboard reveals the menu pill in the top-right. The dash is touch-only by design — no buttons to memorise.
There it is — top right corner. Tap to open the setup menu, or just long-press the RDM logo from the home screen for the same shortcut.

Three actions: Layout picker for switching between saved dashboards, Splash for boot-screen tweaks, and Device Settings for everything else. Up to sixteen layouts are stored on the dash, and the last one you saved loads automatically on power-up.

CAN Bus card. Set the bitrate to match your ECU — 125, 250, 500 or 1000 kilobits — or hit Re-run Auto-Scan and let the dash try them all again. TX and RX frame counters tick alongside so you can see traffic instantly.

Device Info card. Serial number for warranty support, firmware version (currently v1.1.1), MAC address — and the last four hex digits of that MAC are also the suffix on your hotspot SSID, so this is where you go when you've got two RDM-7s in the garage.

Network. Up to five Wi-Fi networks saved with priority by save-order; the dashboard auto-joins whichever's in range. Hotspot is always available alongside as a fallback. Show QR pops a 280-pixel QR code straight onto the screen — point your phone camera at it and Studio opens directly.

Display. Brightness slider runs five to a hundred percent with a live preview. Below it, CAN Dimmer lets you bind brightness to a signal — the headlight bus is the obvious choice, so the dash dims when the lights come on. Night Mode flips the whole layout to a warmer, dimmer palette that doesn't blind you at 2 AM; trigger it manually here, with a two-finger tap on the dash, or auto-bind to the same headlight signal.

Session tools. Data Logging streams every signal to an SD-card CSV at one to two-hundred Hertz, or Max which gets you 70 to 200 Hertz depending on bus load. Peak Hold silently tracks the all-time max and min for every signal — no enable needed. And Testing covers the simulator for bench work plus the signal replayer that pumps a recorded log back through the dash as if it were live CAN.

CAN diagnostics. Live RX and TX counts, error counters, and the bus-state indicator — first place to look if widgets show dashes instead of values. Hit Bus Scan to rerun the bitrate auto-detect from this screen without going back through the wizard.

Footer trio. System Diagnostics opens the five-card health screen — the same one we built up at the bottom of this page. Re-run First-Run Wizard wipes CAN and ECU choices and walks you through setup again. Factory Reset wipes everything — layouts, signals, network — and ships the dash back to brand-new.
Close button up top sends you back to your live dashboard. Every change you made here is already persisted to flash — there's no save step.
And you're back on the road.
